creator: gnupgaug06@oldach.net
text:
Hi Jun,
 
 > At Thu, 3 Aug 2006 12:06:57 +0200 (CEST),
 > Helge Oldach wrote:
 > > The WITH_LIBICONV config knob does not work if libiconv.so is actually
 > > installed on the system. In this case, it is not possible to build gnupg
 > > without using this libiconv.
 > > 
 > > See the patch below for the reason - and fix.
 > 
 > I don't check your patch yet, but can we build gnupg without linking
 > to libiconv with your patch?  Last I tried, configure script detects
 > libiconv automatically and links to it even if I don't want to link
 > with it.
 
 Umm, I overlooked that one. You are right, as ldd reveals.
 
 /usr/local/bin/gpg:
         libiconv.so.3 => /usr/local/lib/libiconv.so.3 (0x28124000)
         libz.so.3 => /lib/libz.so.3 (0x2821a000)
         libbz2.so.2 => /usr/lib/libbz2.so.2 (0x2822a000)
         libreadline.so.6 => /lib/libreadline.so.6 (0x2823b000)
         libc.so.6 => /lib/libc.so.6 (0x28268000)
         libncurses.so.6 => /lib/libncurses.so.6 (0x28345000)
 
 However in that case I think the LIBICONV config knob is completely
 superfluous, and we should use USE_ICONV instead?
 
 Note also that LIBUSB should probably become a config knob.
 
 Regards,
 Helge
